meta.landingTitle: Blueprnt – Job architecture and pay model
meta.landingDescription: Blueprnt builds transparent, scalable models for job architecture and compensation, tailored to Swedish pay equity audits and the EU Pay Transparency Directive.
meta.howDescription: Five steps from zero to a finished job architecture: template, model, blind rating, automatic bands, and calibration with anchor roles.
meta.aboutDescription: Meet the team behind Blueprnt. We build transparent models for job architecture and compensation.

hero.lede: Compare roles for real, based on how your company works. Blueprnt helps you build a job architecture where roles are valued and compared on the same basis across the whole organization.
hero.imageAlt: Isometric visualization of a leveled job architecture

framework.heading: Role evaluation, frameworks, rethought.
framework.lede: We replace ambiguity with a configurable evaluation model. Roles are weighted by complexity, impact, and skill requirements, not tenure, title inflation, or a standard that does not fit your philosophy. This is the model shaped around what your company actually values.

factors.title: Targeted Factors
factors.body: Targeted factors that make clear what the company values in every role: from responsibility and complexity to impact, leadership, and business value.

weighting.title: Guided Weighting
weighting.body: Weighted factors with clear guidance on how each criterion should be rated, for more consistent and accurate evaluations.

structure.title: Automagic Structure
structure.body: Automagic banding that turns a role's overall assessment into the right level: fast, consistent, and free of manual guesswork.

model.titleTail: evaluation model, shaped around what your company actually values.
model.lede: The model is built from your context and what drives your company forward, so the structure becomes clear, relevant, and useful in practice. Easy to get started with, intuitive to use, and free of heavy implementation.

start.label: Feature _A
start.title: Low thresholds, easy start, predictable pricing
start.body: Get going quickly with no long implementation period, no sluggish rollout phase, and no heavyweight project setups.

implementation.label: Feature _B
implementation.title: No lengthy implementation
implementation.body: No large upfront investment, no expensive onboarding fees, a fixed monthly cost, and a discount for longer commitments.

independence.label: Feature _C
independence.title: No dependence on consultants
independence.body: Built to be used without constant consulting support. You can work independently, with help whenever you want it.

exit.label: Feature _D
exit.title: Easy to cancel
exit.body: No needlessly long lock-in, and flexible terms that make it easy to get started.

compliance.title: Support for your pay equity audit, organizational change, role creation, calibration, and more…
compliance.body: Make decisions on a structural foundation, not a guess in a spreadsheet. Everything traces back to role value, ready to be reviewed and defended.

items.directive: The foundation for the EU Pay Transparency Directive

approach.heading: How we work.
approach.lede: One engagement in four steps: simplicity is our guiding principle.

contact.label: STEP_01
contact.title: Contact
contact.body: A first conversation where you present the direction you want to take.

walkthrough.label: STEP_02
walkthrough.title: Walkthrough
walkthrough.body: We guide you through our methodology and setup.

handshake.label: STEP_03
handshake.title: Handshake
handshake.body: You see value in our product, and we see value in you as a customer.

start.label: STEP_04
start.title: Up and running
start.body: You are live in the system and can start exploring the road ahead right away.

contact.heading: Build the structure you deserve.
contact.lede: We are opening up to a limited number of pilot customers at an attractive introductory price. We are looking for small and mid-sized companies that want a clear structure for roles, levels, and pay. Email us a short description of your company, your needs, and what you want to get out of working with us.

how.heading: How Blueprnt works
how.lede: From zero to a finished job architecture in five steps. No consulting hours, no spreadsheet acrobatics, just a clear model the whole organization can trust.

onboarding.title: Onboard in minutes
onboarding.body: Pick an industry template or start from a blank slate. Role families and roles are set up right in the onboarding, pre-filled for your industry.

model.title: Shape the model
model.body: Adjust criteria, rating anchors, and weight points until the model reflects what your company actually values. The weighting always keeps to a fixed point budget, so the whole stays balanced.

rating.title: Rate blind
rating.body: Roles are rated one criterion at a time against clear anchor texts, with scores and bands hidden along the way. That keeps the assessment honest.

bands.title: Bands and results, automatically
bands.body: Scores are recomputed into bands the moment something changes. No manual adjustments, no title inflation, the same basis for every role.

calibration.title: Calibrate with anchor roles
calibration.body: Designate a few reference roles the whole organization agrees on, and let them keep the model honest over time.

about.heading: Who we are.
about.lede: We are a team based in Trollhättan, Sweden, with deep expertise in both HR and software engineering. Our hands-on, theoretical, and strategic experience gives us a unique understanding of the challenges you face every day. With that insight we are now launching blueprnt, the first in a line of innovative solutions set to shape the future of HR work. Our products grow out of real needs and challenges we have experienced first-hand.

story.p1: It started with a simple observation: most companies lack a clear structure for how roles are valued and compared. The result is often a poor fit, you are forced into a standard that does not feel right, and the outcome follows: inequitable, with a culture where we cannot be transparent about how we actually work.
story.p2: We decided to build a tool that turns this around. A platform where every role is assessed against the same objective criteria and all roles can be compared across the whole company, whether you are 20 or 100 people, adapted to your company's unique conditions and values.
story.p3: Today we help small and mid-sized companies lay a structural foundation for their job architecture. We believe in transparency, fairness, and that every employee should understand why their role is valued the way it is.

team.heading: Our team.
team.lede: A small, passionate team with broad experience across HR, tech, product development, and organizational design.

founders.name: The founders
founders.bio: With backgrounds in both tech and HR, we have seen how job architecture can both help and hinder organizations. Blueprnt is our answer.

development.name: The development team
development.bio: We build tools that are as powerful behind the scenes as they are simple to use. Every detail is deliberate.

success.name: Customer success
success.bio: From first contact to finished model, we are at your side. Your success is our success.

cta.heading: Want to know more?
cta.lede: We love talking about job architecture, pay transparency, and how organizations can grow with the right structure. Get in touch!

footer.tagline: Structured role evaluation and banding: the foundation layer for the EU Pay Transparency Directive.

roles.description: Job profiles evaluated against your model. Roles, never persons.

families.heading: Role families and roles at {name}
families.pasteHelpBody: Paste straight from a document, spreadsheet or HR system; the AI works out what you mean. If your text already mentions role families they are kept, otherwise the AI suggests which roles belong together in a family. You review the result and can change everything before anything is created.
families.placeholderPhrase1: Engineering: Software Developer, Tech Lead, Engineering Manager
families.placeholderPhrase2: Accountant, Payroll Specialist, HR Generalist, Office Manager
families.placeholderPhrase3: Store Manager, Sales Associate, Visual Merchandiser
families.reviewDescriptionStarter: A starting point based on your industry. Adjust it freely; everything can be changed later.
families.dndInstructions: Press space or enter to pick up a role, use the arrow keys to move it, and press space or enter again to drop it. Press escape to cancel.
families.reviewHint: Group the roles into families and set each role's track.

model.heading: Choose your evaluation model
model.description: The model defines the criteria roles are rated against. Each criterion gets 1 to 5 weight points within a fixed point budget.

template.title: Start from the standard template
template.description: 9 ready-made criteria with assessment anchors and a balanced weighting. Everything can be adjusted afterwards.

scratch.title: Build from scratch
scratch.description: Start empty and define your own criteria, with AI drafts to help.

review.heading: {name}'s model
review.description: Review the criteria and their weighting. Everything can be changed later.

editor.heading: Criteria
editor.empty: No criteria yet. Add your first criterion or generate AI drafts.
editor.name: Name
editor.pointsLeft: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} left to distribute
editor.pointsOver: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} over the budget
editor.minCriteriaHint: Add at least {min} criteria to continue.
editor.removalFloorHint: A model needs at least {min} criteria, so criteria cannot be removed right now.
editor.addDialogDescription: Describe what the criterion measures and write its assessment anchors. New criteria start at weight 3.
editor.editDialogDescription: Adjust the texts to your organization. The weight and existing ratings are not affected.
editor.removeDialogDescription: The criterion's ratings are removed from every role and its weight points are redistributed across the remaining criteria. This cannot be undone.

ai.heading: AI assistance
ai.provenance: AI-generated suggestion. Review and confirm; nothing is applied automatically.
ai.noMoves: The AI found no moves to suggest.
ai.moveLabel: Move {points, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} from {from} to {to}
ai.reviewLockedHint: The weighting was just reviewed. The review returns when the weighting changes.

help.criterionBody: A criterion is something every role is rated against, like scope or complexity. Anchor texts describe what the ratings 0 to 5 mean for it, and its weight points decide how much it counts in the total score.
help.anchorsBody: Each criterion has six anchor texts describing what the ratings 0 to 5 mean for exactly that criterion. Write them concrete and role-focused, clearly increasing from 0 (not present) to 5 (the strongest reasonable expression), so two assessors reading the same job profile land on the same rating. Assessment anchors are texts on a single criterion; an anchor role is a whole reference role used to compare assessments.
help.anchorRoleBody: An anchor role is a reference role your organization has assessed and agreed on. Use the few you designate (2 to 5 is usually enough) to sanity-check other assessments: the comparison supports a decision, it never replaces the assessment. Anchor roles are whole roles; the assessment anchors on each criterion are descriptions of the ratings.
help.trackBody: A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager. It is set on the role and groups roles with the same kind of responsibility. It says nothing about seniority or how high the role lands.
help.familyBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales. Families organize the role register and the results filter; they never affect the score.
help.weightPointsBody: Every criterion gets 1 to 5 weight points from one fixed budget for the whole model, and the percentage share is computed from the points. Giving one criterion more weight therefore means others count relatively less. New criteria start at the neutral 3.
help.scoreLabel: How are the score and band computed?
help.scoreBody: Every criterion is rated 0 to 5 and weighted by its weight points; the total is normalized to a fixed 0 to 100 scale. The band then follows automatically from the score via the model's band thresholds, where band 1 is the highest. Neither can be set by hand.
help.blindRatingBody: Pick the anchor text that best matches what the role requires, not how the current employee performs. Ratings, score, and band stay hidden until the last criterion so each one is judged on its own; the result appears as soon as every criterion is rated.
help.statusBody: A role moves from draft to in review to approved. Approving locks the profile and the ratings as reviewed; reopen the role to change them again. The status never affects the computed result.
help.familiesReviewBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales; families organize the register and never affect the score. A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager; it says nothing about seniority.
